question.text: What annual compliance requirement is specified under Regulation 12 of SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008?

answer.text: The intermediary must provide SEBI with its CO's certificate on April 1 of each year certifying compliance with all obligations and that all disclosures are true and complete
The intermediary must submit its audited financial statements to SEBI by March 31
The CO must attend a mandatory SEBI compliance training every April
The intermediary must renew its SEBI registration certificate annually

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 12 of SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, the intermediary must provide SEBI with a certificate of its CO on April 1 each year certifying: (a) compliance with all obligations and eligibility criteria on a continuous basis, and (b) that all disclosures in Form A are true and complete. **The Trap:** This is a specific annual certification (not an audit report or training), due on April 1 (not March 31), and must be from the CO specifically. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The CO certificate must also be prominently displayed at all offices including branches, along with the CO's name and contact details for investor grievances.
